history.label: Origins
history.text: The city is possibly named after the Lionga ting assembly which according to Medieval Scandinavian laws was the most important thing in Östergötland.
1082, second in Sweden (within its pre-1658 boundaries) only to Skara.

history.label: 12th century
history.text: The monastery of Vreta Kloster near Roxen north of Linköping was established in 1128, and the oldest parts of the cathedral are also from the 12th century (although it has been changed many times.
On several occasions attempts to achieve a separate Swedish archdiocese were based in Linköping, although, when they finally were successful in 1164, Uppsala was chosen instead.
The coronation of Valdemar Birgersson took place in the cathedral in 1251.
A cathedral school can be traced from 1266.
This ultimately led to the rise to the throne of Charles (de facto at the 1600 Riksdag of the Estates meeting in Linköping and formally four years later) and the end of the short-lived.
In 1627 the current Linköping cathedral school was established, making it the third oldest gymnasium in Sweden.
Linköping was a small town until 1937, when the Saab aircraft industry was formed, starting a period of rapid expansion.
In 2022, a rare Viking grave was found in Linköping that seemed to mark the Viking transition from paganism to Christianity.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: In the reign of Gustavus Vasa several important diets were held in the town.
